prompts: In an electrostatic field, electric field lines originate from positive charges and terminate at negative charges, illustrating the direction of the force experienced by a positive test charge. The density of these lines represents the strength of the electric field; closer lines indicate a stronger field. Around isolated point charges, the lines radiate radially. Between parallel opposite charges, lines run parallel, indicating a uniform field. Near conductors, lines are perpendicular to the surface. These visualizations follow the convention that electric field lines never cross and are closer together where the field is more intense.
